name: Button
description: Triggers an action or event. The primary interactive element for user-initiated actions.

props.name: variant
props.description: Visual style. primary = filled, secondary = outlined/muted fill, ghost = transparent

props.name: size
props.description: Physical size affecting padding and font-size

props.name: loading
props.description: Shows a spinner and prevents interaction while an async action runs

props.name: fullWidth
props.description: Stretches the button to fill its container width

props.name: type
props.description: HTML button type. Use 'submit' inside forms

variants.name: primary
variants.description: High-emphasis action. Use once per section for the single most important action.

variants.name: secondary
variants.description: Medium-emphasis. Supports the primary action or provides a safe alternative.

variants.name: ghost
variants.description: Low-emphasis. Tertiary actions, destructive confirmations, or toolbar controls.

states.name: hover
states.description: Cursor over the button — background shifts one shade darker

states.name: focus-visible
states.description: Keyboard-focused — 2px solid focus ring with offset

states.name: active
states.description: Mouse pressed — slight scale-down for tactile feedback

states.name: disabled
states.description: Interaction blocked — muted palette, not-allowed cursor

states.name: loading
states.description: Async action running — spinner replaces or prefixes label, pointer-events none

do: Use one primary Button per section — it signals the single most important action.
Label with a verb that describes the outcome: 'Save changes', 'Create project', 'Delete account'.
Use loading state for async operations lasting more than 300ms.
Use type='submit' when the button submits a form.
Pair a primary button with a secondary or ghost for a cancel/back option.
dont: Don't use a Button for navigation — use a link (<a>) instead.
Don't stack multiple primary Buttons in the same view.
Don't use disabled to hide unavailable actions — consider explaining why it's unavailable.
Don't truncate button labels — if space is tight, shorten the copy, not the element.
Don't use the ghost variant for the most critical action in a section.

aria: aria-disabled='true' when disabled (keep focusable so screen readers announce it)
No aria-role needed — native <button> has implicit button role

examples.label: Primary variants
examples.description: All three variants at default size

examples.label: Size scale
examples.description: sm / md / lg at primary variant
